expectations.text: Identifies VitisAI as the correct EP for AMD Ryzen AI NPU.
expectations.evidence: Skill body's hardware table specifies VitisAI for Ryzen AI; response uses --ep vitisai / VitisAIExecutionProvider verification.

expectations.text: Recommends a single `winml perf` invocation as the primary action.
expectations.evidence: Single one-shot `winml perf -m facebook/convnext-tiny-224 --device npu --ignore-cache` shown as the 'one command' step.

expectations.text: Does NOT instruct the user to run export, analyze, optimize, quantize, and compile as separate steps.
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly notes 'winml perf builds artifacts on the fly' and avoids manual chaining.

expectations.text: Mentions running `winml inspect` first as a sanity check.
expectations.evidence: Inspect step included as the golden-rule preflight before perf.

expectations.text: Either directs the user to `winml --help`, OR uses only common/plausible flag patterns.
expectations.evidence: All flags real; references `winml <cmd> --help` for fallback diagnostic.

expectations.text: Includes an install/prereq block (or a clear pointer back to install steps) -- the user said "I have a Ryzen AI laptop" with no signal of prior winml usage, so the default-include-install rule applies.
expectations.evidence: Prereq install block included with uv venv + AITK wheel + verify.

expectations.text: All `winml` commands quoted in the response are runnable -- every flag exists in `winml <cmd> --help`, no positional model arg where the CLI is flag-only.
expectations.evidence: All ? winml commands verified runnable against --help.
